title: Of the bold utterance of Trajanus the general.

source.title: NPNF2-03. Theodoret, Jerome, Gennadius, & Rufinus: Historical Writings

verses.text: After Valens had crossed the Bosphorus and come into Thrace he first spent a considerable time at Constantinople, in alarm as to the issue of the war.
He had sent Trajanus in command of troops against the barbarians.
When the general came back beaten, the emperor reviled him sadly, and charged him with infirmity and cowardice.
Boldly, as became a brave man, Trajanus replied: “I have not been beaten, sir, it is thou who hast abandoned the victory by fighting against God and transferring His support to the barbarians.
Attacked by thee He is taking their side, for victory is on God’s side and comes to them whom God leads.
Dost thou not know,” he went on, “whom thou hast expelled from their churches and to whose government these churches have been delivered by thee?”
Arintheus and Victor, generals like Trajanus, confirmed the truth of what he said, and implored the emperor not to be angered by reproaches which were founded upon fact.
